title: RUSTPY-0017 — ctypes simple int/pointer types panic on an out-of-range int (.expect("int too large") in _ctypes/simple.rs)
notes: Systemic .expect() class in _ctypes/simple.rs: c_char_p/c_void_p/c_wchar_p (to_usize().expect('int too large for pointer'), :908/895/921) and c_byte/c_short/c_int/c_long/c_longlong (to_i128().expect('int too large'), :759/775/791/807, unsigned at :767/783/799) all panic on an out-of-range int. CPython MASKS to the C width (c_int(2**200)->0, c_char_p(2**64)->None). Deterministic. Fix = mask like CPython (or raise OverflowError), never .expect(); one sweep of the file fixes the class. Found via c_char_p wrapper in a pure-Python-modules fleet (no --modules-file).
